round1: Round 1 was a brutal reality check for anyone betting on favorites; most of you watched your Bruins, Avs, and Rangers picks go up in smoke. Theodore somehow nailed both major upsets perfectly, while Nathan and Jaclyn proved that, yes, some of you actually pay attention to the game, unlike the majority who picked the Lightning.
round2: Round 2 is over, and for most of you, so are your playoff hopes, thanks to the Leafs and Oilers deciding to pack it in early. Theodore leads with 4 points, while Nickall and Stephanie actually demonstrated some hockey knowledge by nailing Florida and Vegas in the right number of games.
round3: Round 3 is in the books, with Florida sweeping Carolina and Vegas sending Dallas packing in six. Most of you clearly didn't see the Panthers' dominance coming, but at least Nathan, Glenda, Ryan, Benedict, and Robin managed to correctly pick the Golden Knights in six – a feat not shared by those who foolishly put their faith in the Stars or, even worse, the Hurricanes.
round4: The Stanley Cup Final is done, with Vegas taking it in five. Sophie and Benedict actually got it right; the rest of you picking the Knights were just off by a game or two. To everyone else who picked Florida, maybe try predicting a coin toss next time – your odds might improve.
overall: Well, folks, the dust has settled on another NHL playoff season, and the Vegas Golden Knights predictably hoisted the Cup, much to the delight of some and the chagrin of many. Nathan, however, was playing a different game entirely, cruising to victory with a dominant 57 points, clearly either a genius or just incredibly lucky with his 8 correct series picks. On the other end of the spectrum, we have Jake, who, with a pathetic 18 points, proved conclusively that he understands absolutely nothing about hockey; maybe next year try picking teams based on uniform color, it couldn't be worse. Benedict barely managed to cling to second place over Glenda, while Jamie and Marc deserve special mention for their impressive ability to pick only 3 correct games each, a feat of pure anti-hockey intuition. Honestly, some of you should just stick to watching paint dry.
